summary: The `--harness <root>` one-shot run is re-spelled at eight sites, two as byte-equivalent private per-file wrappers — the layer beneath the spawn fold

edit.description: The home. `check_harness` (193) already IS this job — cwd at the harness root, `--harness <root>`, run it — but hardwires `Some("github")` (197) and projects to `(Vec<String>, bool)` (199), so every caller wanting the `CheckRun` itself or a different reporter cannot reach it and re-spells the arg vector against `check_in` instead. Generalize the near-duplicate rather than add beside it (the ladder's third rung): a `CheckRun`-returning home owning the `&["--harness", root.to_str().unwrap()]` construction, with `check_harness` composing it for its github+findings projection — exactly as `check_harness` today composes `check_in` (174) for the spawn and `CheckRun::findings` (157) for the filter. Scope bound, stated so the fold does not over-reach: the home is this ONE arg shape (cwd == root, `--harness <root>`), never a helper per arg permutation; `check_in` stays the general spawn every other shape reaches, and its doc (166-173) gains the new composition step alongside the two it already claims.

`run_check_harness` (95-99) — a private wrapper spelling the arg vector with an `extra` tail, whose `&[]` case is byte-equivalent to `requirement_roster`'s wrapper below; two private names for one job across two files is the signal engineering.md names (shared scaffolding lives in `tests/common`, never copy-pasted per file). Folds onto the home; `check_harness_succeeds` (102) keeps composing it. NOT folded, and the reason is that test's own subject: `check_rejects_a_harness_and_workspace_together` (205) spells `--harness` AND the positional workspace together at 212 with cwd at `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` — the mutually-exclusive-routes usage error, a different run, not this shape.

`check_harness_in` (44-46) — the second private wrapper, `common::check_in(root, &["--harness", root.to_str().unwrap()], None)` at 45, byte-equivalent to `cli.rs`'s `run_check_harness(root, &[])`. Folds onto the home; its doc comment (40-43) carries the surface-present branch THIS file exercises and is the caller's own context, not the home's — keep it at the call site rather than porting it into `tests/common`.

One inline re-spelling at 200 (reporter `None`), folding onto the home. `the_rules_below_the_top_level_are_not_gateable_today` (252) is untouched by this entry — it pins the `(clause-vocabulary-holds)` fork's first instance and its subject is the clause algebra, not the runner.
Two inline re-spellings at 142 and 199 (reporter `None`), both reading `run.ok`/`run.output`. Both fold onto the home.

Two inline re-spellings at 218 and 254, both `Some("github")` reading `run.ok`/`run.output` — the shape that wants the `CheckRun` back, which is exactly why today's `check_harness` (projecting to `(findings, ok)`) could not serve them and the arg vector regrew here. Both fold onto the home.

tests.asserts: no new assertion — this is test scaffolding, and the proof is the existing suite unchanged and green: every folded site keeps its current assertions verbatim, so a behavior change at any of the eight surfaces as a failure rather than a re-blessed expectation

acceptance: Exactly one surface in `tests/` constructs the `--harness <root>` one-shot arg vector, and it lives in `tests/common` (engineering.md, 'One job, one home': test scaffolding lives in one home, never copy-pasted per file; generalize the near-duplicate before adding beside it). `rg '\"--harness\"' tests/` returns the home plus only the two deliberate non-instances named above (`emit.rs`, `cli.rs`'s conflict test). No private per-file wrapper for the shape survives. `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --all --check` green.
notes: Third find in one class: 3bd0f80 homed the harness shape, a9a21a9 the spawn, f9aa32b the finding filter — each left the layer beneath it copied. This is that layer. All eight sites re-read on disk at scoping. Two are deliberately NOT folded (emit.rs:1485 runs from `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` — cwd-independence is its subject; cli.rs:212 is the both-routes usage error), named here rather than left for build to rediscover. scoped at ec2b848.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. All cites re-verified unmoved at ec2b848. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (65) sits under a doc (61-64) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (627) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (654), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 525 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 624 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 649-652 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1356, re-verified unmoved at ec2b848) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1357-1361) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1359) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at ec2b848 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics; inbox empty and the window is tests-only, touching no import mechanics: `git diff 8036900..ec2b848 -- src/` is empty. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` still reads 5 at 65 under a cite claiming five. Stays parked: the constant may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; re-verified ec2b848.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at ec2b848, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. The reconciled window is tests-only and touched no release surface. scoped at b676656.
